---
title: Love Replications Week
date: 2026-03-02
categories:
  - Event
event:
  start_date: '2026-03-02'
  end_date: '2026-03-06'
  description: |
    Granting replications the spotlight for one entire week - learn about best practices, get in touch with experts, and join an open community!

  time: '10:00-17:00'
  location:
    name: Online (Zoom) 
    address: 
    map_url: 
  format:
    type: Online
    detail: ''
  language:
    primary: English
    primary-code: en
    detail: en

links:
  registration: 
  materials: https://zenodo.org/communities/lrw/records?q=&l=list&p=1&s=10&sort=newest
  workshop_website: 
event_description: |
  In the Love Replications Week, learn how to do reproduction and replication studies yourself, submit your own local or virtual events, and show your love for replications — regardless of the type of research that you do!
  
  The OSC was excited to be a partner in this online event where you learn about doing reproduction and replication studies.

  Daily lectures and tutorials to learn how to:
  - Do reproduction and replication studies
  - Choose and justify replication targets
  - Collaborate with students on replication projects
  - Write reproducible reports and get certified reproducible results
  
  [Click here to check out the website](https://forrt.org/LoveReplicationsWeek/) for information on the programme, recordings, and more.
  
instructors:
  - name: 
    url: ''

helpers:
  - name: 
    url: 
  - name:
    url: 
contact:
  name: 
  email: 
organizers:
  - name: Lukas Röseler
presenters:
  - name: 
    url: 
    affiliation: 
  - name: 
    url: 
host:
  - name: Framework for Open and Reproducible Research Training (FORRT)
    url: https://forrt.org/
  - name: German Reproducibility Network (GRN)
    url:
  - name: LMU Open Science Center (OSC)

template: templates/event-template.html
filters:
    - templates/process-event-yaml.lua
---

<!-- Extra content, namely executable R, Python, and Julia code can be added here if desired, and will be rendered at the bottom of the Overview header, but before the presenters/instructors/helpers/contact sections. -->
